text:
There once was a little owl who flew every day. She'd glide through the trees, looking at things. Once she spotted something shiny. An orphan ball! She swooped down and took it home. The next morning, all her owl friends were snacking on the shiny stuff. She wasn't happy about it. She felt lonely.

Soon enough, some human kids arrived near her nest. They were playing, tossing the ball around. "Come play," one boy called out. She felt scared. Their bright looks could scare anyone away! But she had nowhere else to go. She hooted quietly.

The kids looked around for the ball. "Don't worry! We won't take it!" said one. She could believe her luck. She watched them throw the ball back into the forest. They went home laughing. She sat quietly for a while.

Feeling brave, she flew straight back to the ball. Something fluttered atop it. No kids… just a tiny bat?
